questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct reported speech: 'I am tired,' she said.
opcoes: a) She said she is tired.
b) She said she was tired.
c) She said she has been tired.
questoes.explicacao: In reported speech, present simple becomes past simple.

questoes.pergunta: Convert to reported speech: 'I will call you,' he promised.
opcoes: a) He promised he would call me.
b) He promised he will call me.
c) He promised he calls me.
d) He promised he called me.
questoes.explicacao: 'Will' becomes 'would' in reported speech.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct inversion: 'I have never seen such a beautiful sunset.'
opcoes: a) Never I have seen such a beautiful sunset.
b) Never have I seen such a beautiful sunset.
c) Never I saw such a beautiful sunset.
d) Never saw I such a beautiful sunset.
questoes.explicacao: Inversion with negative adverbials: auxiliary + subject.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses inversion correctly?
opcoes: a) Hardly had I arrived when it started to rain.
b) Hardly I had arrived when it started to rain.
c) Hardly had I arrived than it started to rain.
d) Hardly I arrived when it started to rain.
questoes.explicacao: 'Hardly...when' uses inversion: had + subject + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'Only after finishing his work _____ leave.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Only after' at beginning requires inversion: could he.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct conditional: 'If she had studied, she _____ passed.'
questoes.explicacao: Third conditional: would have + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Which is an example of a mixed conditional?
opcoes: a) If I had money, I would buy a car.
b) If I had studied, I would have passed.
c) If I had studied, I would be a doctor now.
d) If I study, I will pass.
questoes.explicacao: Mixed conditional: past condition + present result.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct passive: 'Someone stole my car.'
opcoes: c) My car has been stolen.
questoes.explicacao: Simple past passive: was/were + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Convert to passive: 'They are building a new hospital.'
opcoes: a) A new hospital is being built.
b) A new hospital is built.
c) A new hospital was built.
d) A new hospital has been built.
questoes.explicacao: Present continuous passive: is/are + being + past participle.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses the subjunctive correctly?
opcoes: a) I suggest that he studies harder.
b) I suggest that he study harder.
c) I suggest that he studies harder.
d) I suggest that he is studying harder.
questoes.explicacao: Subjunctive: base form (study) after verbs like suggest.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct form: 'It's important that she _____ on time.'
questoes.explicacao: Subjunctive after adjectives like important: that + subject + base form.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses a reduced relative clause?
opcoes: a) The man who is standing there is my brother.
b) The man standing there is my brother.
c) The man is standing there is my brother.
d) The man who standing there is my brother.
questoes.explicacao: Reduced relative: omit 'who is'.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct participle clause: '_____ by the view, he stopped.'
questoes.explicacao: Past participle (impressed) for passive meaning.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: '_____ the book, she went to sleep.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Having finished' shows sequence (first finished, then slept).

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses a cleft sentence?
opcoes: a) It was John who broke the window.
c) The window was broken by John.
d) Who broke the window was John.
questoes.explicacao: Cleft: 'It was ... that/who' emphasizes a part.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'What I need _____ a break.'
questoes.explicacao: Subject = 'What I need' (singular), so 'is'.

questoes.pergunta: Complete with a causative: 'I _____ my car repaired.'
questoes.explicacao: Causative: have/get something done.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses 'wish' correctly for the past?
opcoes: a) I wish I studied harder.
b) I wish I had studied harder.
c) I wish I would study harder.
d) I wish I study harder.
questoes.explicacao: Wish + past perfect for past regrets.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'If only I _____ more time.'
questoes.explicacao: 'If only' + past simple for present wishes.

questoes.pergunta: Which is a correct example of ellipsis?
opcoes: a) She can play the piano, and he can too.
b) She can play the piano, and he can play the piano too.
c) She can play the piano, and he too.
d) She can play the piano, and he can also.
questoes.explicacao: Ellipsis omits repeated words: 'and he can (play) too'.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'He speaks French, and _____ .'
questoes.explicacao: Inversion with 'so' for agreement: so + auxiliary + subject.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'I don't like coffee, and _____ .'
questoes.explicacao: 'Neither' + auxiliary + subject for negative agreement.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses 'it' as a dummy subject?
questoes.explicacao: 'It' in weather expressions is dummy (no referent).

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'There _____ a book and two pens on the table.'
questoes.explicacao: 'There is' + singular first item (book) is often used, though formal grammar might use 'are' for plural complement. In speech, 'is' is common.

questoes.pergunta: Which is a correctly formed tag question?
opcoes: a) You are tired, aren't you?
b) You are tired, are you?
c) You are tired, isn't it?
d) You are tired, don't you?
questoes.explicacao: Positive statement, negative tag: aren't you?

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'Let's go, _____ ?'
questoes.explicacao: Tag for 'let's' is 'shall we'.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'I'm late, _____ ?'
questoes.explicacao: 'Aren't I' is the accepted tag for 'I am'.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence has a gerund as subject?
opcoes: b) To swim is good exercise.
d) Swimming every day, he stayed fit.
questoes.explicacao: Gerund (swimming) as subject.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct infinitive: 'She made him _____ the dishes.'
questoes.explicacao: Bare infinitive after 'make'.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'He is known _____ a great artist.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Known to be' is correct.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses 'having + past participle' correctly?
opcoes: a) Having finished his work, he went home.
b) Having finish his work, he went home.
c) Having been finished his work, he went home.
d) He having finished his work, went home.
questoes.explicacao: Perfect participle shows completed action before main verb.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'Not _____ what to do, she called for help.'
questoes.explicacao: Negative participle: Not knowing...

questoes.pergunta: Which is a correct example of a compound adjective?
questoes.explicacao: Compound adjectives before nouns are hyphenated.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'He is _____ to win the race.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Likely' is an adjective here, followed by infinitive.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'The meeting _____ postponed.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Is likely to be' is correct.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses 'would rather' correctly?
opcoes: a) I would rather stay home than go out.
b) I would rather to stay home than go out.
c) I would rather staying home than going out.
d) I would rather stay home than to go out.
questoes.explicacao: 'Would rather' + base form + than + base form.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'It's time you _____ to bed.'
questoes.explicacao: 'It's time' + past subjunctive (went) for present/future meaning.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'She acted as if she _____ the boss.'
questoes.explicacao: 'As if' + past subjunctive (were) for unreal situation.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses a preposition at the end naturally?
opcoes: a) This is the book which I was looking for.
b) This is the book for which I was looking.
questoes.explicacao: Both are acceptable; ending with preposition is common in informal English.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'Not only _____ late, but he also forgot the documents.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Not only' at beginning requires inversion: was he.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: '_____ in the rain, she got wet.'
questoes.explicacao: Present participle for simultaneous action.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence uses a reflexive pronoun correctly?
questoes.explicacao: Both positions are correct for emphasis.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'Each of the students _____ a book.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Each' takes a singular verb.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'The number of applicants _____ increasing.'
questoes.explicacao: 'The number of' is singular.

questoes.pergunta: Which sentence is correct?
opcoes: a) A number of issues has been resolved.
b) A number of issues have been resolved.
c) The number of issues have been resolved.
d) A number of issues has been resolved.
questoes.explicacao: 'A number of' is plural.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: 'Neither the teacher nor the students _____ happy.'
questoes.explicacao: With 'neither...nor', verb agrees with the closest subject (students = plural).

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'Either you or I _____ wrong.'
questoes.explicacao: Verb agrees with closest subject: I (am).

questoes.pergunta: Which is a correct example of a participle clause?
opcoes: a) Walking home, the rain started.
b) Walking home, I got wet.
c) Walked home, I got wet.
d) To walk home, I got wet.
questoes.explicacao: The subject of the participle must match the main clause subject.

questoes.pergunta: Complete: '_____ by the news, she burst into tears.'
questoes.explicacao: Past participle for passive meaning.

questoes.pergunta: Choose the correct: 'I have never seen her again, nor _____ to.'
questoes.explicacao: 'Nor' + inversion: nor do I want.
